summary: Iteration 90 - User-settable @username field feature fully tested. Backend: 12/12 tests passed. Frontend: Registration form shows optional username field with @ prefix and helper text. Profile page shows username field with @ prefix, edit capability, and helper text. All validations working correctly.
passed_tests: BACKEND: GET /api/customer/profile returns username field
BACKEND: PUT /api/customer/profile with valid username succeeds
BACKEND: PUT /api/customer/profile with username <3 chars returns 400
BACKEND: PUT /api/customer/profile with invalid chars (!@-#$) returns 400
BACKEND: PUT /api/customer/profile with spaces auto-strips them
BACKEND: PUT /api/customer/profile accepts alphanumeric + underscores + dots
BACKEND: PUT /api/customer/profile with @prefix auto-strips the @
BACKEND: PUT /api/customer/profile with username >30 chars returns 400
BACKEND: Username is converted to lowercase
BACKEND: Duplicate username for different user returns 400
BACKEND: User can keep their own username (no false uniqueness error)
BACKEND: Registration endpoint accepts optional username field
FRONTEND: Registration form shows Username field (not marked as required)
FRONTEND: Registration form shows @ prefix inside username input
FRONTEND: Registration form shows helper text 'Opcional. Si no lo eliges, se generara automaticamente.'
FRONTEND: Profile page shows Username field under Personal Information
FRONTEND: Profile page shows @ prefix inside username input
FRONTEND: Profile page shows helper text 'Letras, numeros, puntos y guiones bajos. Min 3 caracteres.'
FRONTEND: Profile username can be edited and saved
FRONTEND: Toast notification appears after saving username
FRONTEND: Username persists correctly after page reload
critical_code_review_comments: Good implementation - username validation logic in customer.py handles all edge cases
Spaces are stripped (not rejected) which is a valid design choice - 'test user' becomes 'testuser'
@ prefix auto-stripping works correctly - users can type @username or username
Uniqueness check properly excludes current user to allow keeping same username
Frontend uses data-testid attributes correctly for testing
Helper text provides clear guidance on allowed characters
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 90 tested the user-settable @username feature. Backend endpoint PUT /api/customer/profile accepts 'username' field with validations: min 3 chars, max 30 chars, only alphanumeric + underscores + dots, auto-lowercase, auto-strip @ prefix and spaces. Uniqueness enforced across users. Frontend: Registration page at /signup has optional username field. Profile page at /dashboard/profile shows username under Personal Information. Test user test@example.com has username 'test_ui_fa5a'.
